title: Docker Run
content:
### The `docker run` Command

`docker run` creates and starts a container from an image. It's the most frequently used Docker command.

### Common Flags

| Flag | Description |
|---|---|
| `-d` / `--detach` | Run container in background (detached mode) |
| `--name` | Assign a name to the container |
| `--rm` | Automatically remove container when it exits |
| `-i` / `--interactive` | Keep STDIN open (used with -t) |
| `-t` / `--tty` | Allocate a pseudo-TTY (used with -i) |

### Basic Examples

```bash
# Run in foreground
docker run nginx

# Run in background
docker run -d nginx

# Run with a name
docker run -d --name my-nginx nginx

# Interactive terminal (e.g., for a shell)
docker run -it ubuntu bash

# Auto-remove after exit
docker run --rm -it ubuntu bash
```

### Managing Container State

```bash
docker start my-nginx    # start a stopped container
docker stop my-nginx     # gracefully stop a running container
docker restart my-nginx  # restart a container
docker pause my-nginx    # pause all processes
docker unpause my-nginx  # unpause processes
```

### Restart Policies

Use `--restart` to control automatic restart behavior:

| Policy | Behavior |
|---|---|
| `no` | Never restart (default) |
| `always` | Always restart unless explicitly stopped |
| `unless-stopped` | Restart unless manually stopped |
| `on-failure` | Restart only if container exits with non-zero code |

```bash
docker run -d --restart always nginx
```

### Resource Limits

Control CPU and memory usage:

```bash
docker run -d --memory=512m --cpus=1.5 nginx
docker run -d --memory=256m --memory-swap=512m nginx
```

- `--memory` sets the hard memory limit
- `--memory-swap` sets the total (memory + swap)
- `--cpus` limits CPU cores (1.5 = one and a half cores)

quiz.question: Which flag runs a container in the background?
What does the `--restart always` policy do?
options: Restarts the container only if it crashes
Always restarts the container even after manual stop
Always restarts the container unless manually stopped
